data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T172809-7a0a7018/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T172809-7a0a7018/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T172809-7a0a7018/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T172809-7a0a7018/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T172809-7a0a7018/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-2d49729e">
    <title>work: stop queue drain on resource_exhausted attempts</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
ddx work currently continues scanning and claiming ready beads after setup/infrastructure failures. During the May 6 ENOSPC incident, ddx work failed to create one isolated worktree, then immediately claimed another production-reachability bead and failed again. Resource exhaustion must stop the drain because continuing expands the blast radius.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 initializes loop events and exitReason but does not include resource_exhausted as a recognized loop-fatal exit reason.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:529 onward continues iterating after non-success reports unless existing retry/no-progress/once logic stops it; there is no host-resource circuit breaker.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:263-283 defines status taxonomy around execution_failed/no_evidence/etc., but there is no distinct resource_exhausted status/reason for loop policy to inspect.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:434-440 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:281-284 require ddx work to stop claiming new beads after resource_exhausted.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a stable resource_exhausted attempt/loop classification that can be returned by ddx try/resource preflight and recognized by ExecuteBeadWorker.
- On resource_exhausted, release any non-terminal claim, emit cleanup/resource events, set loop.end exit_reason=resource_exhausted, and return without scanning the next ready bead.
- Print an operator-visible message such as resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop.
- Ensure resource_exhausted is not counted as model no-progress, capability retry, or review/quota retry.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the resource checker itself here; this bead consumes the outcome from the try/resource-preflight bead.
- Do not change queue ranking or production-reachability bead priority.
- Do not change provider 429 in-attempt retry behavior.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedStopsLoop creates two ready beads and an executor returning resource_exhausted for the first; verifies the second bead is never claimed or executed.
2. TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedUnclaimsAndNoCooldown verifies any claimed bead is open/re-claimable and no execute-loop-retry-after/no-progress cooldown is written.
3. TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhaustedLoopEndEvent verifies loop.end includes exit_reason=resource_exhausted and cleanup/resource detail is emitted.
4. TestWorkResourceExhausted_PrintsOperatorMessage verifies the ddx work command output includes resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop or the finalized exact message.
5. Resource exhaustion remains distinct from review transport/quota and provider rate-limit retry in status/detail handling.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run "TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhausted|TestWorkResourceExhausted" -count=1 passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:cli, kind:bug, reliability, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025, resource-exhaustion, cleanup</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-f3dfde0d" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="8d52f6a27e0e8100bb348d727c28d14a5dd0ad37" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T172809-7a0a7018"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `32489438e` (`fix: stop queue drain on resource exhaustion [ddx-2d49729e]`).

Key changes:
- Added a stable `resource_exhausted` status and stop message in [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-2d49729e-20260506T172809-7a0a7018/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go#L47).
- Made `ExecuteBeadWithConfig` return a structured resource-exhausted result instead of a hard error in [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-2d49729e-20260506T172809-7a0a7018/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go#L695).
- Taught the execute loop to unclaim, emit resource cleanup/detail events, log the operator message, and exit before scanning another bead in [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-2d49729e-20260506T172809-7a0a7018/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go#L862).
- Plumbed the same status through `ddx work` and printed the operator-visible stop message in [`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-2d49729e-20260506T172809-7a0a7018/cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go#L1740) and [`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-2d49729e-20260506T172809-7a0a7018/cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go#L1828).
- Added focused regression tests in [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_resource_exhausted_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-2d49729e-20260506T172809-7a0a7018/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_resource_exhausted_test.go#L52) and [`cli/cmd/work_resource_exhausted_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-2d49729e-20260506T172809-7a0a7018/cli/cmd/work_resource_exhausted_test.go#L56).
- Updated the existing resource preflight test to expect the structured result in [`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_resource_preflight_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-2d49729e-20260506T172809-7a0a7018/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_resource_preflight_test.go#L89).

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run "TestExecuteBeadWorkerResourceExhausted|TestWorkResourceExhausted" -count=1`
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passed during the commit flow.
